creator: ipfreak@yahoo.com
text:
---- in rc.conf, sendmail parameters were set as following:

sendmail_enable="NO"
sendmail_submit_enable="NO"
sendmail_outbound_enable="NO"
sendmail_msp_queue_enable="NO"

---- sendmail daemon was not running:

user@server:~:$ sudo service ssendmail status
ssendmail does not exist in /etc/rc.d or the local startup
directories (/usr/local/etc/rc.d), or is not executable

---- but everyday morning 03:00, there were following messages logged in console.log:

Mar 6 03:01:00 <console.info> server kernel: Mar 6 03:01:00 <mail.crit> server sendmail[35319]: My unqualified host name (server) unknown; sleeping for retry
Mar 6 03:02:00 <console.info> server kernel: Mar 6 03:02:00 <mail.alert> server sendmail[35319]: unable to qualify my own domain name (server) -- using short name
Mar 6 03:02:01 <console.info> server kernel: Mar 6 03:02:01 <mail.crit> server sendmail[44481]: My unqualified host name (server) unknown; sleeping for retry

---- per advice, changed following lines in periodic.conf as workaround:

daily_clean_hoststat_enable="NO"
daily_status_mailq_enable="NO"
daily_status_mail_rejects_enable="NO"
daily_queuerun_enable="NO"

---- discussion thread:

https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/how-to-stop-sendmail-100.79186/
